creator: rene@FreeBSD.org
text:
The Fedora Core 4 ports expired when FreeBSD 7.X reached end-of-life. This patch removes these ports and updates bsd.port.mk, bsd.linux-apps.mk, and bsd.linux-rpm.mk. This patch should be a no-op on FreeBSD 8 and higher.

While Linux kernel 2.4.2 is still supported in the kernel, there is no base port that supports it any longer, so remove support for it from bsd.*.mk.

* Fix some hardcoded dependencies in multimedia/linux-ymessenger 

* Make Fedora Core 10 the new default for USE_LINUX=yes

* Change _GL_linux_RUN_DEPENDS in bsd.port.mk to use graphics/linux-dri74 instead of graphics/linux_dri, which seems the default for Fedora Core 10

* Pet portlint for the linux-base ports.

A local INDEX-9 build with the patch applied succeeded.

Note that bsd.linux-rpm.mk now has some wrong indentation, the indentation was left unaltered to make the changes more visible.

How-To-Repeat: Apply patch at
ftp://rene-ladan.nl/pub/freebsd/linuxports-cleanup.r313966.diff

SHA256 (linuxports-cleanup.r313966.diff) = a735b721135a8fc18e006a64b7d7d7ccb9ccadaea52c19263a3a093fc46bd7b1
-rw-r--r--  1 rene  ftp  650914 Mar 12 12:37 linuxports-cleanup.r313966.diff


For readability, there is also a patch generated with "svn --no-diff-deleted":
ftp://rene-ladan.nl/pub/freebsd/linuxports-cleanup-ndd.r313966.diff

SHA256 (linuxports-cleanup-ndd.r313966.diff) = 6d663196e5183116d0305dbf7d851e6b9c15cc7f23dd0babfd3c44dfef7743a0
-rw-r--r--  1 rene  ftp   70871 Mar 12 12:37 linuxports-cleanup-ndd.r313966.diff
